summary: Backend fully functional (10/10 pytest passing) covering auth for 3 seeded roles, forms CRUD, role-based status flow User->Supervisor->HRDD, unauthorized access, and stats. Frontend login/routing works, Montserrat font is applied globally. CRITICAL BUG: html2pdf.js is referenced via window.html2pdf() in Forms.js but the library is never loaded (no CDN script in public/index.html and no npm import) - PDF generation for both Nomination and Job Analysis forms will fail with 'window.html2pdf is not a function'.

minor.issue: allow_origins includes '*' together with allow_credentials=True - browsers will reject credentialed requests with wildcard; works now only because preview URL is also listed. Should restrict to explicit FRONTEND_URL.
Supervisor query returns {} (all forms) per comment 'simplified'; not scoped by department as intended.

ui_bugs.issue: window.html2pdf is undefined - html2pdf.js library not loaded. Verified via page.evaluate('typeof window.html2pdf') returned 'undefined'. PDF Print button will throw runtime error.
ui_bugs.selector: button with Printer icon in view modal

integration_issues.issue: html2pdf library missing; clicking 'Print PDF' throws TypeError and never downloads. Fix: add <script src='https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2pdf.js/0.10.1/html2pdf.bundle.min.js'></script> to /app/frontend/public/index.html OR `yarn add html2pdf.js` and `import html2pdf from 'html2pdf.js'`.

issues: Good Montserrat font, clean login22-style layout

User role only sees Dashboard + My Forms & Nominations (correct). Need to verify Supervisor/HRDD sidebar shows 'Pending Approvals' nav item - not tested due to token limits but E1 should verify DashboardLayout role-conditional rendering.

action_items: CRITICAL: Load html2pdf.js library. Add CDN script tag in /app/frontend/public/index.html before </body>: <script src='https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2pdf.js/0.10.1/html2pdf.bundle.min.js'></script>
Verify Supervisor & HRDD sidebar shows 'Pending Approvals' link and the view=='approvals' route renders correctly in DashboardLayout/DashboardHome
Tighten CORS: remove '*' when allow_credentials=True; list only preview + localhost origins
Supervisor form filter should be department/dept-scoped, not return all forms
critical_code_review_comments: Forms.js: `f.status.includes('pending')` etc. will throw if any form has null status - defensive check needed
Forms.js generatePDF mutates hidden DOM by id before render - if multiple simultaneous PDFs or re-renders happen, stale data may appear
server.py CORS misuse: '*' + allow_credentials=True is invalid per spec
server.py: using @app.on_event('startup') is deprecated in FastAPI; use lifespan context
Forms.js handleStatusUpdate: no optimistic UI, no confirmation dialog before Approve/Reject
test_credentials: admin@example.com/admin123 (HRDD), supervisor@example.com/super123 (Supervisor), user@example.com/user123 (User) - all verified working
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 3 role accounts on startup; test_form_submit_get_and_status_flow creates TEST_-prefixed nomination forms (not cleaned up - minor).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully tested via /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (10 tests, all pass). Frontend: login + dashboard verified for User role. NOT yet tested: Supervisor/HRDD dashboards & approvals flow, PDF generation end-to-end (blocked by html2pdf missing), role-based sidebar. After E1 adds html2pdf script, re-test: (1) login as user -> create nomination -> open view modal -> click Print PDF -> download triggered; (2) login as supervisor -> Pending Approvals tab visible -> approve user's form -> status becomes pending_hrdd; (3) login as admin/HRDD -> see pending_hrdd form -> approve -> status becomes approved.
rca of the issue: PDF generation bug RCA: Forms.js line 91 calls window.html2pdf() but grep shows no script tag loading html2pdf.js anywhere in /app/frontend/public/ or any import in /app/frontend/src/. Verified in-browser: typeof window.html2pdf === 'undefined'. Mitigation: add CDN script (fastest) or yarn add html2pdf.js and import.
